targetSentence: How are you?

sentence: How are you? I’m ____ , thanks.
hint: How are you 用来询问对方情况，回答时可以用 fine, thanks 这样的简单表达。

audioText: How are you?

scenario: 你第一次见到一位同学。对方对你说 “Hello! How are you?” 你想礼貌地回应。
pragmaticsNote: Use a short, polite answer to How are you? The best response is I'm fine, thanks.

audioText: 发音：/heˈloʊ/；意思是“你好”。
hint: 这是第一次见面时最常用的打招呼用语，拼写以 h 开头。

sourceText: 你好吗？
acceptedAnswers: How are you?
How are you doing?
hint: 把中文问候语翻译成英文，常用表达是 How are you?

sourceText: 有人问你情况时，你可以说：“我很好，谢谢。”

targetSentence: He is a new student.

sentence: ___ am a student.
hint: 主语代词 I 后面要用 am，表示“我是……”。

audioText: I am a student.

sourceText: 大家好，我叫李明。我是一名新学生。
options: Hello, everyone. My name is Li Ming. I am a teacher.
Hello, everyone. My name is Li Ming. He is a new student.
Hi, everyone. I am Li Ming. We are new students.
Hello, everyone. My name is Li Ming. I am a new student.

sourceText: 我是学生。
acceptedAnswers: I am a student.
I'm a student.
I am student.
hint: 用 I am 表达“我是……”。student 前面通常要加 a。

audioText: I am a student. She is a teacher.
question: 你听到的内容最接近哪一项？

scenario: 在课堂上，老师请你介绍自己，并说你旁边的同学是新朋友。
options: Hello, I am Mei. She are my friend.
Hello, you are Mei. He is a student.
Hello, I am Mei. I am a student. This is my new friend, Tom.
pragmaticsNote: Option 1 is best because it correctly uses I am to introduce yourself and this is to introduce another person. It is clear, polite, and suitable for a classroom introduction.

audioText: 听音并拼写：/ˈstuː.dənt/。意思：在学校里学习的人。
hint: 请拼写表示“学生”的英语单词。注意中间是 -dent，不要漏掉字母 d。

tags: my your his her our their

sentence: I fill in ____ information on this form.
hint: 这里要用物主形容词，表示“我的”。物主形容词要放在名词前面，例如 my information。

targetSentence: Your name is on the form.

audioText: This is my form.
question: 听句子，选择正确意思。

sourceText: 这是我的个人信息表，请填写你的信息。
options: This is his personal information form. Please fill in your information.
This is your personal information form. Please fill in my information.
This is our personal information form. Please fill in their information.
This is my personal information form. Please fill in your information.

audioText: 听发音并拼写：/ðeər/，意思是“他们的”。
hint: 这是一个表示所属关系的形容词，常用于“their form”这样的表达。

scenario: 你在课堂上填写个人信息表，老师说：“Please fill in your name.” 你想告诉老师这是你的表格。
pragmaticsNote: Use my to show that the form belongs to the speaker.

sentence: _____ your book and read the lesson.
hint: 这里用祈使句，动词原形放在句首，表示老师的指令。

targetSentence: Listen and read.

sourceText: 请打开书，读一读，然后大声说出来。
options: Open your book, read it, and say it aloud.
Sit down, listen carefully, and spell the word.
Open your notebook, ask a question, and answer.
Close your book, read it, and speak quietly.

audioText: Open your book, please.
question: 老师说了什么？

sourceText: 请听。
acceptedAnswers: Listen.
Please listen.
Listen, please.
hint: 把中文课堂指令翻译成英文祈使句，句子可以很简短。

audioText: 老师说：请听。
hint: 意思是“听；注意听老师说的话”。请拼写这个课堂指令单词。

scenario: 老师在课堂上说：“Open your book, please.” 你听懂了，想礼貌回应。你应该怎么说？
pragmaticsNote: A short polite reply like “Okay.” shows that you understand the instruction and are ready to do it.

audioText: a pencil

sentence: ___ is your name?
hint: 用 What 询问名字或物品名称；用 Who 询问人或身份。

audioText: What is this? It is a pencil.
question: 录音里说的是什么？

sourceText: 谁有一支笔？

targetSentence: What is a dictionary?

audioText: 听音并拼写：你在课本或书里查单词意思时会用到的东西。
hint: 这是一个单数名词，前面可以用 a。意思是“词典”。

scenario: 在教室里，老师举起一本词典问你这是什么，你要用英语回答。
pragmaticsNote: Use 'It's a/an ...' to answer a question about an object. 'Who' is for people, not things.

sourceText: 这是什么？这是一支钢笔。她是谁？她是安。
acceptedAnswers: What is this? It is a pen. Who is she? She is Ann.
What's this? It's a pen. Who is she? She's Ann.
What is this? It's a pen. Who is she? She is Ann.
hint: 注意 what 用来问事物，who 用来问人；单数可数名词前常用 a 或 an。

audioText: This is my father's photo.
question: 听句子，选择正确的中文意思。

sentence: My ______ name is Anna.
hint: 名词所有格 's 表示“……的”，如 my mother's name = 我妈妈的名字。

sourceText: 这是我妈妈的照片。她有两个孩子。
options: This is my mother photo. She has two children.
This is my father's photo. She has two children.
This is my mother's photo. She has two children.
This is my mother's photo. She has two child.

targetSentence: My aunt's children are my cousins.

sourceText: 我妈妈的名字是安娜。
acceptedAnswers: My mother's name is Anna.
My mom's name is Anna.
My mum's name is Anna.
hint: Use possessive 's to show a family relationship.

audioText: 发音：/ˈtʃɪldrən/；意思：孩子们。
hint: 这是 child 的复数形式。注意拼写里有 -ren，不要写成 childrens。

scenario: 你正在向朋友介绍一张家庭照片。朋友指着照片里的一位男士问：“Who is he?” 你想说他是你妈妈的哥哥。
pragmaticsNote: The first answer correctly uses possessive 's to show the family relationship and fits the situation. The other two are grammatically or logically wrong.

targetSentence: My friends are ten years old.

sentence: My friend is __ years old.
hint: 表示年龄时，常用“be 动词 + 数字 + years old”。这里要填表示年龄的数字 ten。

sourceText: 这是我的同学，他十岁了。
options: This is my classmate. He is five.
These are my classmates. He are ten.
This is my teacher. He is ten.
This is my classmate. He is ten.

audioText: 听到的单词：/ˈpɜːrsən/。请根据发音和意思拼写这个单词。
hint: 意思是“一个人；一个个体”。注意：单数形式是 person，复数形式是 people。

audioText: I have two friends. They are ten years old.
question: 你听到的内容是哪一项？
options: 我有两个朋友，他们十岁。
我有三位老师，他们十岁。

sourceText: 这是我的朋友李明。他是一个学生。他十岁。他出生于2015年。
acceptedAnswers: This is my friend Li Ming. He is a student. He is ten years old. He was born in 2015.
This is my friend, Li Ming. He is a student. He is ten years old. He was born in 2015.
This is my friend Li Ming. He is a student and he is ten years old. He was born in 2015.
hint: 注意用 be 动词表达年龄：He is ten years old。表达出生年份可以用 He was born in 2015。

audioText: There are three people. They are my friends. They are ten years old.

sourceText: 她有一头长发和大眼睛。
acceptedAnswers: She has got long hair and big eyes.
She has long hair and big eyes.
She's got long hair and big eyes.
hint: 注意用 have got / has got 表示“有”，并把形容词放在名词前面，例如 long hair, big eyes。

audioText: 听发音并拼写：表示“漂亮的；美丽的”，常用来形容人或外貌。
hint: 请根据读音和中文意思写出这个形容词。

audioText: She has got big eyes and a small nose.
question: 听句子，选择正确的一项。

sourceText: 我的朋友有大眼睛和小鼻子。
options: My friend has got small eyes and a big nose.
My friend is got big eyes and a small nose.
My friend has big eyes and small nose.
My friend has got big eyes and a small nose.

scenario: 你的同学请你描述你的朋友 Lily。你想说她有大眼睛、短头发，而且很漂亮。你应该怎么回答？
options: She has got big eyes and short hair. She is my sister.
She have got big eyes and short hair. She is beautiful.
She has got big eyes and short hair. She is beautiful.
pragmaticsNote: Option 1 is the best response because it correctly describes appearance with have got and places adjectives before nouns. Option 2 has a grammar error, and option 3 is grammatically correct but does not answer the request to describe her appearance.

sentence: Look at that photo. _____ is my sister in the pink dress.

sentence: I am ___ because I have got no breakfast.
hint: 注意：be 动词 am/is/are 后面可以接形容词来表示感觉或状态。这里“没有早餐”表示“饿”，所以要选 hungry。

sourceText: 我现在又累又饿。
options: I have tired and hungry now.
I am thirsty and bored now.
I am tired and hungry now.
I am happy and excited now.

audioText: I am thirsty.
question: 听句子，选出正确的中文意思。

targetSentence: I am thirsty.

scenario: 你看到朋友刚做完运动，满头大汗，看起来很累。你想礼貌地问他是不是口渴。
pragmaticsNote: The best reply uses 'Are you ...?' to ask about another person's feeling. 'Thirsty' is an adjective, so we do not say 'have thirsty'.

audioText: 听到的词：表示“开心”的形容词，读音类似 /ˈhæpi/。
hint: 这是一个表示情绪的形容词，意思是“开心”。请拼写 5 个字母。

sourceText: 我很开心。
acceptedAnswers: I am happy.
I'm happy.
I am very happy.
hint: Use be + adjective. Both 'I am happy.' and 'I'm happy.' are correct.

options: My phone number is three two five. My room number is thirteen. It costs eleven dollars.
My phone number is three two five. My room number is twelve. It costs ten dollars.

My phone number is three five two. My room number is twenty. It costs ten dollars.

hint: 这是一个基数词，表示 13。注意它和 thirty 的拼写不要混淆。

sentence: The cup costs ___ dollars.

audioText: My phone number is three two one, four five six, seven eight zero.
question: 你听到了哪个电话号码？

scenario: 一位新朋友问你：“What is your phone number?” 你要慢慢说出数字。
options: My phone number is three dollars.
My phone number is one-two-three, four-five-six, seven-eight-nine-zero.
My phone number is on the phone.
pragmaticsNote: The best response clearly gives the phone number in a simple, spoken number sequence.

sourceText: 我的电话号码是二三一，四五六七。
acceptedAnswers: My phone number is two three one, four five six seven.
My telephone number is two three one, four five six seven.
hint: Say the numbers one by one.

sentence: The movie starts __ 8:00 tonight.
hint: 表示具体钟点时，通常用 at，例如 at 8:00。

audioText: A: When does the movie start? B: It starts at seven o'clock tonight.
question: 电影什么时候开始？

targetSentence: When does the lesson start?

audioText: 听发音：/əˈklɒk/；意思是“整点”，常和数字连用，如 seven o'clock。
hint: 这个词表示“整点”，写法里有一个撇号，常见于时间表达。

sourceText: 我们今晚七点见面。

scenario: 你在和朋友确认今晚电影开始时间。朋友问你：“电影什么时候开始？” 你想用最自然的方式回答开始时间。
options: It starts in seven o’clock tonight.
It starts on seven o’clock tonight.
It starts at seven o’clock tonight.
pragmaticsNote: Use at with a specific time. The best reply answers the question directly and gives the exact start time.

sourceText: 会议在七点开始。
acceptedAnswers: The meeting starts at seven o'clock.
The meeting begins at seven o'clock.
It starts at seven o'clock.
hint: 时间前面用 at 表示具体时间，比如 at seven o'clock。

targetSentence: I wake up at seven and eat breakfast.

sentence: I ______ breakfast at seven.
hint: 表示日常习惯时，主语是 I，动词用原形。

sourceText: 我七点起床，八点去上学。
options: I get up at seven and go to school at eight.
I get up at seven and go to bed at eight.
I get up at seven and have dinner at eight.
I wake up at seven and study at eight.

audioText: I wake up at seven and eat breakfast at eight.
question: 他说了什么？
options: 他七点起床，八点吃早餐。
他七点上学，八点做作业。
他七点睡觉，八点吃午饭。

scenario: 你的同学问你：“When do you wake up and go to school?” 你想用日常习惯来回答。
options: I am waking up at seven and go to school at eight.
I wake up at seven and go to school at eight.
I wake up at seven and went to school at eight.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply uses the present simple to describe a regular daily routine.

audioText: 听音并拼写：/ˈbrekfəst/，意思是“早餐”。
hint: 这是每天早上吃的第一餐，常用在日常作息中。

sourceText: 我每天七点起床，然后吃早餐。
acceptedAnswers: I wake up at seven every day and then eat breakfast.
I get up at seven every day and then eat breakfast.
Every day, I wake up at seven and eat breakfast.
hint: 注意用一般现在时描述习惯；wake up 和 get up 都可以表示“起床”。

sentence: I ____ study English on Monday.
hint: 频率副词 usually 表示“通常”，一般放在实义动词 study 前面。

targetSentence: I usually study on Monday and play sports on Saturday.

sourceText: 我通常在星期一学习，星期三锻炼，周末和家人一起活动。
options: I usually study in Monday, exercise on Wednesday, and spend time with my family on the weekend.
I always study on Monday, exercise on Wednesday, and spend time with my family on the weekend.
I usually study on Tuesday, exercise on Wednesday, and spend time with my family on the weekend.
I usually study on Monday, exercise on Wednesday, and spend time with my family on the weekend.

audioText: I usually study English on Monday and Wednesday. I always do sports on Saturday. On Sunday, I never work.
question: 根据录音，什么时候他做运动？

scenario: 你的老师问你这一周怎么安排学习和运动。你想说你通常在工作日学习，周末有时运动。
options: I always study in Monday and Sunday, and I never exercise on the weekend.
I usually study on Monday, on Tuesday, on Wednesday, on Thursday, and on Friday. I sometimes exercise on Saturday and on Sunday.
I usually study in Monday to Friday, and I sometimes exercise at weekend.
pragmaticsNote: The best response clearly matches the weekly plan and uses correct frequency adverbs and the preposition on for days of the week.

audioText: 听音并拼写：表示“通常；一般来说”的频率副词。
hint: 这是一个表示频率的副词，常用于说一周里的学习、工作或运动安排。

sourceText: 我通常在星期一学习英语，星期二去工作，星期六运动，星期天休息。
acceptedAnswers: I usually study English on Monday, go to work on Tuesday, exercise on Saturday, and rest on Sunday.
I usually study English on Monday, work on Tuesday, exercise on Saturday, and rest on Sunday.
I usually study English on Monday, go to work on Tuesday, do exercise on Saturday, and relax on Sunday.
hint: 注意用 usually 表示“通常”，并用 on + 星期几 表示具体某一天。

sentence: My birthday is on the ____ of May.
hint: 这里用序数词表示日期顺序。日期前常用 on，像 the fifth of May 表示“5月5日”。

targetSentence: My birthday is in the fifth of April.

sourceText: 你生日是几月？

audioText: When is your birthday? My birthday is in July. It is on the fifth day of the month.
question: 她的生日在哪一个月？

scenario: 你想问同学的生日是哪一天。你应该怎么说？
pragmaticsNote: Use When is your birthday? to ask about a birthday date or month. What is your birthday? is not natural English, and My birthday is in April. does not ask a question.

audioText: 听录音，拼写表示“二月”的英文单词。
hint: 这是一个月份名，开头是 F，结尾是 -ary。

sourceText: 我的生日在四月。
acceptedAnswers: My birthday is in April.
My birthday falls in April.
hint: 表示月份时，通常用 in，例如 in April。

targetSentence: There is a bathroom in my apartment.

audioText: There are two rooms and a kitchen in my apartment.
question: 录音里说公寓里有什么？
options: 我的公寓里有一个花园和三个浴室。
我的公寓楼有五层，没有房间。
我的公寓里有两个房间和一个厨房。

sourceText: 我的公寓里有两个房间、一个浴室和一个厨房。
options: There are two rooms, a bathroom, and a kitchen in my building.
There is a room, a bathroom, and a kitchen in my apartment.
There are two rooms, a bathroom, and a kitchen in my apartment.
There is two rooms, a bathroom, and a kitchen in my apartment.

audioText: 请根据读音 /ˈwɪndoʊ/ 写出这个单词。
hint: 意思：房间里可以看见外面的玻璃窗。

sourceText: 我的公寓里有一个厨房和两个房间。
acceptedAnswers: There is a kitchen and there are two rooms in my apartment.
My apartment has a kitchen and two rooms.
There is a kitchen in my apartment, and there are two rooms.
hint: 注意单数用 There is，复数用 There are。

scenario: 你在介绍自己的公寓。朋友问你家里有哪些房间。你想用正确的句子回答。
options: There are two rooms and a bathroom.
There is two rooms and a bathroom.
There are a room and a bathroom.
pragmaticsNote: Use There are with plural nouns like two rooms. The other options sound incorrect.

unitTheme: Furniture and Things in a Room

audioText: There are some cups on the table.

sentence: The bag is ____ the table.

sourceText: 包在桌子下面。
options: The bag is on the table.
The bag is next to the table.
The bag is behind the table.
The bag is under the table.

audioText: Open the door.
question: 你听到了什么指令？

sourceText: 请把门打开。

sentence: ___ here and sit down.
hint: 祈使句通常直接使用动词原形来发出指令或请求，这里空格应填动词原形 Come。

sourceText: 把包放在桌上。
acceptedAnswers: Put the bag on the table.
Place the bag on the table.
Put your bag on the table.
hint: 使用动词原形开头，表示简单指令，例如 Put the bag on the table.

scenario: 你在客厅里对朋友说：请把包放到桌子上，然后坐在椅子上。你想听到一个自然的英语回应。
options: Okay. I'll put the bag on the table and sit on the chair.
Okay. Put the bag on the table and sit on the chair.
Yes, the bag is big and the chair is white.
pragmaticsNote: The best response accepts the instruction and shows that the person will do the actions.

audioText: 请听并拼写：表示“把某物拿到说话者这里”的动词。
hint: 这是祈使句里常用的动词原形，意思是把某物带过来。

sentence: I have ____ for breakfast.

I have one egg and some bread for breakfast.
hint: Use 'an' with 'egg' and 'some' with 'bread'.

audioText: 听录音并根据释义拼写：一种常见的午餐食物，通常用两片面包夹着食物。
hint: 这是一个可数名词；注意不要把中间的字母顺序写错。

scenario: 你在早餐时想告诉朋友你吃了面包和一个鸡蛋。朋友问你：“What do you eat for breakfast?” 你想自然地回答。
options: I eat some bread and some egg for breakfast.
I eat some bread and an egg for breakfast.
I eat a bread and some eggs for breakfast.
pragmaticsNote: Option 1 is the most natural and correct answer because bread is uncountable, so we use some bread, and egg is countable, so we use an egg. The other options sound unnatural because they use the wrong quantity words.

targetSentence: I would like an apple and a cup of tea.

sourceText: 我想点一杯咖啡和一块蛋糕。
options: Could I have a tea and a slice of cake?
Could I have some coffee and some cake?
Could I have a coffee and a slice of cake?
Could I have coffees and cakes?

targetSentence: I like hot soup when I am hungry.

sourceText: 我更喜欢热饮，不喜欢冷饮；我现在很口渴。
options: I prefer hot drinks. I do not like cold drinks. I am thirsty now.
I like cold drinks. I love sweet food. I am full now.
I want hot drinks, but I choose cold drinks. I am hungry now.
I enjoy cold drinks. My favourite food is soup. I am not thirsty.

audioText: I love hot soup. I prefer warm tea, and I do not like cold drinks. I am hungry.
question: 他说自己更喜欢什么？

sentence: I ___ spicy food because it tastes bad to me.
hint: 注意：主语是 I 时，否定形式用 do not like，表示“不喜欢”。第三人称单数才用 does not like。

sourceText: 我喜欢热咖啡，但是我不喜欢冷饮。
acceptedAnswers: I like hot coffee, but I do not like cold drinks.
I like hot coffee, but I don't like cold drinks.
I love hot coffee, but I do not like cold drinks.
hint: Use I like..., I love..., and I do not like... to talk about food and drinks.

scenario: 你的朋友问你要不要喝咖啡。你不喜欢咖啡，但你更想喝茶。你会怎么回答？
options: Coffee is bad. I want a cold apple.
I don’t like coffee. I prefer tea.
Yes, I love coffee, and I am full.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply says what you like and what you prefer. The other options are unnatural or do not fit the situation.

audioText: 听发音并拼写：/ˈfeɪ.və.rət/，意思是“最喜欢的”。
hint: 表示“最喜欢的；最喜爱的”。请写出这个单词的正确拼写。

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely

pairs.left: I would like a cup of tea, please.
Can I have a glass of water, please?

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
sentence: ___ a cup of tea, please?
hint: 用 Can I have...? 礼貌地向服务员提出请求，后面接你想要的食物或饮料。

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
targetSentence: I would like a cup of tea, please.

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
sourceText: 我想要一杯咖啡，请给我菜单。
options: Can you give me a bottle of coffee? I like this dish.
I would like a cup of tea, please. Can I have the bill?
I would like a cup of coffee, please. Can I have the menu?
I want two glasses of coffee, and please bring the table.

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
sourceText: 请给我一杯水。
acceptedAnswers: Can I have a glass of water, please?
I would like a glass of water, please.
I'd like a glass of water, please.
hint: 点餐时可以用 I would like... 或 Can I have...? 来礼貌地请求。

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
scenario: 你在咖啡馆看菜单，想礼貌地点一杯茶。服务员站在桌边，你要向他提出请求。
options: Can I have a cup of tea, please?
Give me a cup of tea.
pragmaticsNote: Use "Can I have...?" to make a polite request. The other options are not polite or do not work as requests.

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
audioText: 你会听到 /pliːz/，这是点餐时常用的礼貌用语。
hint: 意思是“请；劳驾”，常用于向服务员礼貌提出请求。

unitTheme: Reading a Menu and Ordering Politely
audioText: I would like a glass of water, please.
question: 听句子，选择正确的意思。

sentence: How much ____ a cup of coffee?
hint: 询问价格时，How much 后面常用 is，表示“多少钱”。

sourceText: 这个咖啡多少钱？
options: How many coffees do you want?
How much coffee do you want?

audioText: 请拼写：用于询问价格或不可数名词数量的英文短语。
hint: 这个短语常用于问价格，比如在咖啡馆问饮料多少钱。

audioText: Customer: How much is the sandwich? Server: It is five dollars. Customer: How many pieces of cake do you have? Server: We have three pieces.
question: 顾客问了什么？

scenario: 你在咖啡馆点餐，想先问一杯咖啡多少钱，再确认纸杯蛋糕有几个。你应该说什么？
options: How much is the coffee, and how much cupcakes are there?
How many is the coffee, and how much cupcakes are there?
How much is the coffee, and how many cupcakes are there?
pragmaticsNote: Use How much to ask about price, and How many to ask about countable items.

sourceText: 这些三明治多少钱？我想买三个。
acceptedAnswers: How much are these sandwiches? I want to buy three.
How much do these sandwiches cost? I want three.
How much are these sandwiches? I’d like three, please.
hint: Translate the price question and the quantity request into natural English.

targetSentence: I want to buy these jeans.

sourceText: 请让我看一下那件裙子。
options: Can I have a look at that dress?
Can I have a look at this dress?
Can I have a look at that skirt?
Can I have a look at these dresses?

sourceText: 我想看这件衬衫和那顶帽子。
acceptedAnswers: I want to see this shirt and that hat.
I'd like to see this shirt and that hat.
I want to look at this shirt and that hat.
hint: 注意指示代词：this 表示“这”，that 表示“那”；单数名词前分别用 this/that。

audioText: Hello. I want to try on these shoes.
question: 他说想试穿什么？

scenario: 你在服装店里，看见柜台后面有两顶帽子。你想请店员拿给你看。
options: Can I see that hat, please?
Can I see this hats, please?
Can I see those hats, please?
pragmaticsNote: Use those for plural items that are farther away. The request is polite and matches the two hats in the shop.

audioText: 听音并拼写：一件商店里常见的外套，穿在上身，通常比 coat 更轻。
hint: 这是一件上身穿的衣服，常见于服装店，通常比外套更轻。
在购物场景中，this/that 用于单数名词，these/those 用于复数名词。这里 shoes 是复数，所以要用 these 或 those；表示离自己较近、想试穿的物品时通常用 these。
sentence: I want to try ____ black shoes.

audioText: This blue shirt.

I’d like to try this blue sweater, please.
pragmaticsNote: Use this + a singular noun to refer to the near blue sweater politely.

targetSentence: This coat is more expensive than that coat.

sourceText: 这件 T 恤比那件更大，也更贵。
options: This T-shirt is the same size as that one, but newer.
This T-shirt is smaller and more expensive than that one.
That T-shirt is bigger and cheaper than this one.
This T-shirt is bigger and more expensive than that one.

sentence: The red shirt is ___ than the blue shirt.
hint: 比较两个东西时，形容词要用比较级，常用结构是 A + be + 比较级 + than + B。这里表示“更大”，所以用 bigger。

audioText: The blue shirt is bigger than the red shirt, but the red shirt is more expensive.
question: 哪件衬衫更大？

sourceText: 这件外套比那件更大，也更贵。
acceptedAnswers: This coat is bigger than that one and more expensive.
This coat is larger than that one and more expensive.
This coat is bigger and more expensive than that one.
hint: 用比较级 bigger / larger 和 more expensive，常用 than 来比较两件衣服。

audioText: 听录音：/ˈbɪɡər/。意思：更大的。请拼写这个比较级单词。
hint: 这是 big 的比较级，表示“更大”。

scenario: 你在试穿两件T恤。店员问你哪一件更大、更新或更贵。你想清楚地比较这两件衣服。
options: It is big, so I buy two.
This one is bigger and more expensive than that one.
I want the same shirt, but in red.
pragmaticsNote: The best response clearly compares size and price using comparative adjectives.

targetSentence: This shirt is too big, but this one is good enough.

audioText: This T-shirt is too small for me.

sentence: This coat is warm ___ for winter. I want to wear it.
hint: “enough” 放在形容词后面，表示“足够……”，如 warm enough；“too” 放在形容词前面，表示“太……”，如 too big。

sourceText: 这件毛衣对我来说够暖和，不过太大了。你有小一号的吗？
options: This sweater is warm enough, but it is a little too big. Do you have a smaller size?
This sweater is warm enough, and it is perfect. I really like this style.
This sweater is too big, but it is not beautiful.
This sweater is too warm and too small. Do you have a bigger size?

audioText: 发音：/ɪˈnʌf/。听这个单词，并拼写出来。
hint: 意思是“足够；够了”。常用于 good enough、warm enough 这样的表达。

sourceText: 这件衬衫对我来说太小了。你有更大的尺寸吗？
acceptedAnswers: This shirt is too small for me. Do you have a bigger size?
This shirt is too small for me. Do you have a larger size?
This shirt is too small for me. Do you have another size?
hint: 注意用 too small 表示“太小了”，用 bigger size 或 larger size 表示“更大的尺寸”。

audioText: This blue sweater is too small. The grey sweater is warm enough, and it looks really nice. It is my favourite.
question: 说话者觉得哪件毛衣最合适？

scenario: 你在试衣间试穿一件衬衫。店员问你：“How does it fit?” 你觉得它太小了，想要大一号。
options: It's too big enough, but the style is my favourite.
It's good enough. I really like the design, so I will take it.
It's too small. Do you have a bigger size?
pragmaticsNote: Option 1 is best because it clearly says the clothes are too small and politely asks for a bigger size.

audioText: Can you show them to me?

pairs.left: Can you show them to me?
I want to pay by card.

sentence: Can you show ___ the price of this red shirt?
hint: 这里要用宾格作宾语。show someone something 结构中，someone 用宾格；句子里需要 me，而不是 I、my 或 mine。

imageDesc: 顾客在商店收银台前拿着卡准备付款，店员在旁边等着刷卡。
imagePrompt: a customer at a shop counter holding a payment card, cashier behind the counter, price tag on a shirt, shopping bag on the table, simple white background
imageSvg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512"><rect width="512" height="512" fill="white"/><rect x="70" y="260" width="372" height="110" rx="12" fill="#d9e3f0"/><rect x="105" y="175" width="110" height="85" rx="10" fill="#f2c14e"/><circle cx="160" cy="140" r="34" fill="#8ecae6"/><rect x="145" y="174" width="30" height="90" fill="#219ebc"/><rect x="300" y="165" width="130" height="160" rx="14" fill="#ffb703"/><rect x="335" y="205" width="58" height="34" rx="5" fill="#1d3557"/><text x="364" y="228" font-size="16" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">CARD</text><circle cx="250" cy="220" r="30" fill="#cdb4db"/><rect x="233" y="250" width="34" height="72" fill="#8d99ae"/><rect x="195" y="290" width="110" height="22" rx="6" fill="#adb5bd"/><text x="250" y="284" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">shop</text></svg>

targetSentence: Can you give us the change, please?

sourceText: 请把那个商品给我看看。它多少钱？我可以用卡付款吗？
options: Could you sell me that product? How much money did I spend? Can I pay with cash only?
Can you show us those products? How many are they? Can we change the card?
Please give them to me. How much do they buy? Can I cost it by money?
Could you show me that product? How much does it cost? Can I pay by card?

audioText: 请听音并拼写：/tʃeɪndʒ/（在商店付款后拿回来的钱）
hint: 意思：付款后店员找给你的钱；在句子里常见于“找零”这个场景。

audioText: Hello. I want to buy these socks. Can you show them to me? They cost five dollars. You can pay with a card, and the change is one dollar.
question: 这双袜子多少钱？

sourceText: 你能把那些衣服给我看一下吗？它们多少钱？
acceptedAnswers: Can you show me those clothes? How much do they cost?
Can you show those clothes to me? How much do they cost?
Can I see those clothes? How much are they?
hint: 注意宾语代词的位置：me 通常放在动词后或介词 to 后，them 可以指前面提到的东西。

scenario: 你在商店里买两件毛衣。店员说总价是30美元。你想礼貌地请他把这两件商品拿给你看。
options: Can you show me to them?
Can you show them to me?
pragmaticsNote: Use them for the two sweaters and me for the customer.

targetSentence: Could I have a bag, please?

audioText: Could I have a bag, please?
question: 听录音，选择说话者在请求什么。

sourceText: 我可以要一个袋子吗？
options: Can you help me find some bread?
Could I have a bag, please?
Could you give me a bottle, please?

sentence: _____ I have a bag, please?
hint: 在礼貌请求时，Could 比 Can 更客气；句型是 Could I + 动词原形 + ...?

pairs.left: Could I have a bottle of milk?
Can you help me find the apples?
Could you tell me how many boxes I need?
pairs.right: 你能告诉我我需要几个盒子吗？

scenario: 你在超市买了 bread、milk 和 apples，想礼貌地请店员帮你拿一个袋子。你应该怎么说？
options: Could I have a bag, please?
Can I have milk and apples?
pragmaticsNote: The correct reply uses a polite request with "Could I... please?". Option 2 is too direct, and option 3 does not ask for help with the bag.

audioText: 发音：/kʊd/。释义：礼貌请求时用的词，意思是“可以；能否”。
hint: 这个词常用于礼貌请求，比如 “Could I have a bag?”。请拼出这个词。

sourceText: 你能帮我拿一个袋子吗？
acceptedAnswers: Can you help me get a bag?
Could you help me get a bag?
Can you help me with a bag?
hint: 用 Can you... ? 或 Could you... ? 礼貌地请求帮助。

sentence: ___ can I get to the museum?
hint: 用 how 询问到达方式；用 where 询问地点。这个句子是在问“我怎么去博物馆？”

targetSentence: Where is the museum?

sourceText: 请问，酒店在哪里？我怎么去那里？
options: Excuse me, could you tell me where the hotel is and how I can get there?
Excuse me, could you tell me what the hotel is and when it opens?
Excuse me, could you tell me where the hotel was and how I got there?
Excuse me, could you tell me which hotel is the biggest in this area?

audioText: Excuse me, where is the hotel?
question: 听句子，选择正确答案。

sourceText: 酒店在哪里？
acceptedAnswers: Where is the hotel?
Where's the hotel?
Where is the hotel located?
hint: 用 where 询问地点；句子要自然、简单。

scenario: 你在城市里找一家 hotel，想先问它在哪里，再问怎么去那里。
pragmaticsNote: Use 'where' to ask about a place. Use 'how' to ask about the way to get there.

audioText: 听发音：/mjuːˈziːəm/
hint: 城市里可以参观展品、学习历史和艺术的地方。

audioText: Turn left.

sentence: ____ the street and turn right.
hint: 祈使句常用动词原形开头来给出方向；这里需要选择表示“穿过/横穿”的短语。

sourceText: 请左转，然后穿过这条街，去车站。
options: Turn right, then go across the street to the station.
Turn left, then go across the street to the station.
Walk around the street and go to the station.
Go straight, then turn left at the station.

targetSentence: Turn left at the station.

audioText: Walk across the street and turn left. The station is next to the bank.
question: 他说了什么？
options: 穿过街道，然后左转。车站在银行旁边。
沿着河边一直走到公园。

scenario: 你在城市街道上问路。对方说：“Go across the street, then turn right and walk to the station.” 你想礼貌地确认自己听懂了。
options: No, I will go to the west tomorrow.
Thank you. Go across the street and turn right.
The station is next to my house.
pragmaticsNote: The best response is a polite confirmation that shows understanding of the directions.

audioText: 听音：/left/。这个词表示“左边；向左”。

sourceText: 向左转，然后走到车站。
acceptedAnswers: Turn left, then walk to the station.
Turn left and walk to the station.
Turn left, then go to the station.
hint: Use an imperative sentence to give directions in natural English.

sentence: I go to the station ___ every morning.
hint: “by + 交通工具” 用来表示出行方式，例如 by bus、by taxi、by car、by plane。

targetSentence: I go to school by bus.

audioText: I go to the station by bus.
question: 她是怎么去车站的？

sourceText: 我通常坐公交车去车站，因为开车会很堵。
options: I usually go to the station by bus because driving is slow in traffic.
I usually go to the station by bike because the road is very long.
I usually go to the station by car because driving is very fast.
I usually go to the station by plane because it is a short trip.

audioText: I go to the station by bus.

sourceText: 我坐公交车去学校。
acceptedAnswers: I go to school by bus.
I take the bus to school.
hint: 用 by + 交通工具 表达出行方式；go to school 表示“去学校”。

scenario: 你在车站。朋友问你怎么去学校。你想说你坐公交车去。
pragmaticsNote: The best reply uses 'by' + transport to say how you travel. The other options sound unnatural or incomplete.

audioText: 听音：/ˈbaɪ.sɪ.kəl/；释义：自行车
hint: 这是表示“自行车”的英文单词。请注意拼写，末尾是 -cycle，不是 -cyle。

sentence: The visitor walks ___ the airport building to show a passport.
hint: 表示进入建筑物时，用 into。to 表示去某地，through 表示穿过，across 表示横穿。

sourceText: 我去机场问信息，走进大楼，然后出示我的护照。
options: I go to the airport, ask for information, walk into the building, and show my passport.
I go into the airport, ask for a form, walk across the building, and show my camera.
I go to the beach, ask for information, and show my passport.
I return to the airport, ask for information, and walk through the door with my bag.

targetSentence: The tourist goes through the door and walks to the information desk

audioText: 听音：[ˈpɑːspɔːt]；释义：在机场出示的旅行证件
hint: 请根据发音和释义，拼写出这个单词。它表示你在机场需要出示的旅行证件。

audioText: Please walk into the building and go to the information desk with your passport.
question: 你听到的是哪一句？
options: 请穿过海滩，去海边的大船。
请走进大楼，带着护照去信息台。
请把相机放进袋子里，然后返回国家。

scenario: 你在机场柜台前。工作人员请你出示护照和填写好的表格，并告诉你去信息台问登机口。你想礼貌地回应并说明你会去信息台。
options: Give me your phone and camera. I do not need the form.
I want to go through the beach and return the capital.
Yes, here is my passport and form. I will go to the information desk now.
pragmaticsNote: The best response is polite and fits the airport situation. It mentions the passport and form, and it says the speaker will go to the information desk.

sourceText: 请带上护照去机场，走进大楼，然后去信息台。
acceptedAnswers: Please take your passport to the airport, walk into the building, and go to the information desk.
Take your passport to the airport, walk into the building, and go to the information desk.
Please take your passport to the airport and walk into the building, then go to the information desk.
hint: Use movement prepositions naturally: use 'to' for a destination and 'into' for entering a place.

audioText: We are going to stay in a hotel this weekend.

audioText: We are going to stay in a hotel this weekend. I am going to call the hotel and ask for a room with a bathroom.
question: 这段话里，他们要做什么？
options: 他们这个周末要住酒店，还要打电话要一个带浴室的房间。
他们要去商店买新衣服。

sentence: We are going to book a room on the ____ for the weekend.
hint: 这里用 be going to 表示已经计划好的未来动作；空格里要选表示“网站”的词。

audioText: 听音：/vəˈkeɪʃn/；释义：假期，常用于周末或旅行时的休息时间。请拼写这个单词。
hint: 提示：这个词表示“假期”，和 weekend 旅行很相关。注意中间是 -ca-，结尾是 -tion。

sourceText: 我们准备这个周末住在一家酒店，并去参观一个博物馆。
acceptedAnswers: We are going to stay in a hotel this weekend and visit a museum.
We're going to stay in a hotel this weekend and visit a museum.
We are going to stay in a hotel and visit a museum this weekend.

scenario: 你在酒店前台办理入住。工作人员问你周末有什么计划。你要用英语回答。
options: We are going to stay in a hotel and visit museum.
We stay in a hotel and visit a museum tomorrow.
We are going to stay in a hotel and visit a museum.
pragmaticsNote: The best answer uses be going to to talk about a simple future plan and gives a clear, natural reply to the hotel staff.

sentence: I ____ science and art at university.
hint: 一般现在时用来描述日常学习安排；主语是 I 时，动词用原形。

targetSentence: I study history in the library after class.

audioText: I study at the university in the morning, and I have a history lesson in the afternoon.
question: 你听到的句子是什么意思？
options: 我早上在大学学习，下午有历史课。
我在学校吃午饭，晚上学习语言。
我早上在图书馆工作，下午上科学课。

sourceText: 我在大学学习历史和英语，每天有两节课。
options: I study history and English in the university, and I have two lessons every day.
I am studying history and English at university, and I have two classes every day.
I work history and English at university, and I have two classes every day.
I study history and English at university, and I have two classes every day.

audioText: 听音并拼写：表示“大学”的单词。
hint: 请拼写这个表示“大学”的英语单词，注意中间的字母组合和重音位置。

scenario: 你在课堂上向新同学介绍自己。对方问你：“你在哪所学校学习？你每天学什么？”
options: I study at college. I have English and history classes every day.
My school is near my home, and I like it very much.
I am studying at college now. English and history are good.
pragmaticsNote: The best response uses the present simple to describe school and daily classes clearly.

sourceText: 我是一名大学生。我每天早上在学校上课，学习历史和科学，下午在图书馆做作业。
acceptedAnswers: I am a university student. I have classes at school every morning. I study history and science, and I do my homework in the library in the afternoon.
I am a college student. Every morning, I go to class at school. I study history and science, and I do homework in the library in the afternoon.
I am a university student. I attend classes at school every morning. I study history and science, and I do my homework in the library in the afternoon.
hint: Use the present simple to describe a daily study routine.

pairs.left: I have to do my homework.
You have to write one paragraph.
The teacher says we have to use a dictionary.
pairs.right: 老师说我们必须使用词典。

sourceText: 老师说你必须在明天前交阅读作业。
options: You have to hand in the reading homework by tomorrow.
You must start the reading homework tomorrow.
You have to read the homework before tomorrow.
You can hand in the reading homework tomorrow.

targetSentence: You have to read the page and write a paragraph.

audioText: You must complete the homework before the exam.
question: 听句子后，选择正确意思。
options: 你必须在考试前完成作业。

audioText: 请听单词读音：/ˈdɪfɪkəlt/。意思是：不容易的；难的。
hint: 这个形容词常用来描述作业、考试或阅读材料很难。

sentence: I ___ finish my homework before the exam.
hint: have to 表示“必须/不得不”，后面接动词原形。这里说的是现在的学习任务，所以用 have to。

sourceText: 你必须在明天前完成这篇文章的阅读和写作练习。
acceptedAnswers: You must finish the reading and writing exercises for this article by tomorrow.
You have to finish the reading and writing exercises for this article by tomorrow.
You must complete the reading and writing practice for this article by tomorrow.
hint: 注意用 must 或 have to 表达“必须”，并保留“明天前”和“阅读和写作练习”的意思。

scenario: 老师说你明天有 exam，今晚必须复习，还要完成一篇 article 的阅读和一段 writing 作业。你想告诉朋友你今晚要做什么。
options: I don't have to read the article because the exam is easy.
I have to read the article and write one paragraph tonight.
I must to read the article and write one paragraph tonight.
pragmaticsNote: The best response clearly uses 'have to' to show a necessary homework task. The other options are wrong because one has incorrect grammar and the other does not fit the situation.

sentence: My sister is a nurse. She works in a hospital and ____ patients every day.
hint: 一般现在时中，当主语是第三人称单数（如 she, he, my sister）时，动词通常要加 -s。

sourceText: 她在医院工作，是一名护士。
options: She works in a company. She is a teacher.
She works at a hospital. She is a nurse.
She works in an office. She is a doctor.
She works on a farm. She is an artist.

audioText: My mother is a doctor. She works in a hospital.
question: 听句子，选出正确的一项。
options: 她是一名医生，在医院工作。
她是一名农民，在农场工作。
她是一名老师，在学校工作。

targetSentence: A doctor works at a hospital.

sourceText: 老师在学校工作。她每天教学生。
acceptedAnswers: The teacher works at a school. She teaches students every day.
A teacher works at school. She teaches students every day.
The teacher works in a school. She teaches students every day.
hint: Use the present simple to describe a job, a workplace, and a daily action.

audioText: 听这个读音：/ˈtiː.tʃər/
hint: 意思：在学校工作，教学生的人。

scenario: 你在和新朋友聊天。对方问你：你的妈妈在哪里工作？她做什么工作？
options: She works at a hospital. She is a nurse.
She is a nurse, and she works yesterday.
She works at a school. She is a teacher.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply uses the present simple to say where she works and what her job is.

audioText: 你听到的单词是 /ˈɪntərvjuː/，意思是“面试”。请拼写这个单词。
hint: 表示“面试”的英文单词，常用于工作或学校的简单交流。

sourceText: 你为什么来参加这个面试？
acceptedAnswers: Why are you here for the interview?
Why did you come to the interview?
Why are you coming to this interview?
hint: Use why to ask for the reason. Keep the sentence simple and natural in English.

audioText: Why do you want this job?
question: 听句子，选择正确的中文意思。
options: 你为什么想要这份工作？

scenario: 你在参加简单面试。面试官问你：'Why do you want this job?' 你需要选择最合适的回答。
options: Because I am interested in this work and I want to learn new skills.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply answers the question directly and gives a clear reason with because. The other options do not answer the interviewer properly.

sentence: I can sing, and I want to learn to ___ the piano.
hint: can 表示会做某事；want to learn to 后面接动词原形，表示“想学习做……”。

targetSentence: I cannot draw, but I want to learn to paint.

audioText: I can draw, but I cannot paint. I want to learn to cook.
question: 他说了什么？
options: 他不会画画，也不会做饭；他想忘记单词。
他会弹钢琴，也会唱歌；他想学跳舞。
他会画画，但不会画画；他想学做饭。

sourceText: 我会画画，但我不会唱歌。我想学会唱歌。
options: I can draw, but I can't sing. I want to learn how to sing.
I can paint, but I can't sing. I want to learn how to sing.
I can draw, but I can't dance. I want to learn how to sing.
I can draw, but I can't sing. I want to learn how to dance.

sourceText: 我会弹吉他，但我不会画画。我想学会画画。
acceptedAnswers: I can play the guitar, but I cannot draw. I want to learn to draw.
I can play guitar, but I can't draw. I want to learn to draw.
I can play the guitar, but I can't draw. I want to learn how to draw.
hint: 注意用 can / can't 表达会做或不会做的事情；用 want to 和 learn to 表达想学习的目标。

audioText: 听发音：这个词表示“电脑”，是一种对学习和工作都很 useful 的东西。
hint: 这个词的意思是“电脑”；常和 internet 一起使用。

scenario: 老师问你会不会画画。你现在不会，但你想学画画。你应该怎么回答？
options: I can draw now, but I do not want to learn to draw.
I cannot draw now, and I want to cook.
I cannot draw now, but I want to learn to draw.
pragmaticsNote: The best response clearly says you cannot do it now and states your learning goal with want to learn to.

audioText: I enjoy reading books.

targetSentence: I like reading in my free time.

sentence: My sister loves ______ in her free time.
hint: like / love / enjoy 后面通常接动词的 -ing 形式，例如 love dancing。

sourceText: 我喜欢周末读书，也喜欢画画。
options: I love dancing on weekends, and I enjoy writing.
I enjoy reading books, but I don't like painting.
I like watching TV on weekends, and I enjoy cooking.
I enjoy reading on weekends, and I like painting too.

scenario: 你的朋友问你：“你平时喜欢做什么？” 你想自然地回答自己喜欢阅读和画画。
pragmaticsNote: The best reply is a natural habit statement with like + -ing. Option 2 sounds like actions happening right now, not a hobby. Option 3 is grammatically incorrect.

audioText: I like reading in my free time.
question: 录音里的人喜欢做什么？

audioText: 听音：读作 /ˈriːdɪŋ/
hint: 这个单词表示“阅读”；它是一种爱好，常和 like、love、enjoy 连用，例如 I like reading.

sourceText: 我喜欢读书。
acceptedAnswers: I like reading.
I love reading.
I enjoy reading.
hint: 把“喜欢”翻译成 like、love 或 enjoy，并把动词变成 -ing 形式。

sourceText: 我每周去健身房做运动，有时周末去游泳。
acceptedAnswers: I go to the gym to exercise every week, and sometimes I go swimming at the weekend.
I go to the gym every week to do exercise, and sometimes I go swimming on weekends.
I exercise at the gym every week, and sometimes I go swimming on weekends.
hint: 注意常见搭配：go to the gym, do exercise, go swimming。

scenario: 你的朋友说他今天打 tennis 打得不好，很失望。你想礼貌地安慰他，并鼓励他下次继续练习。
options: Don't worry. You will play well next time.
Yes, you are bad. Stop playing.
pragmaticsNote: The best response is kind and encouraging. Option 1 supports the friend and fits the situation. Option 2 is rude, and Option 3 does not answer the friend properly.

sourceText: 她每周去游泳池游泳，游得很好。
options: She goes swimming at the pool every week and swims well.
She goes swimming at the pool every week and plays well.
She does swimming at the pool every week and swims badly.
She plays swimming at the pool every week and swims well.

sentence: I want to go to the concert, ___ I have no ticket.
hint: but 用来表示转折，前后意思相反；这里表示“想去音乐会”，但“没有票”。

audioText: I like the band and the singer.
question: 他说他喜欢什么？

audioText: 发音：/ˈsɪŋər/；意思：唱歌的人
hint: 请根据发音和意思，拼写表示“唱歌的人”的英语单词。

targetSentence: I like the singer and the band because the song sounds good.

imageDesc: 一群人在室内看现场歌手演出，前面有舞台和麦克风。
imagePrompt: crowd watching a live singer on a stage, microphone, guitar, indoor hall, tickets at the entrance

sourceText: 我很喜欢这支乐队。周六晚上有一场音乐会，你想和我一起去吗？
options: I really like this band. There is a concert on Saturday night. Would you like to come with me?
I really like this band. There is a concert on Saturday morning. Do you want to buy a ticket?
I really like this band, but there is no concert on Saturday night. Would you like to stay home?
I really like this singer. There is a festival on Saturday night. Would you like to come with me?

scenario: 你的朋友邀请你今晚去听一个音乐会，他说有一个你喜欢的歌手和乐队表演。你想礼貌地接受邀请并问一些简单信息。
options: Maybe we can buy a piano instead.
I'd love to go. What time does the concert start?
No, I don't like music, so don't ask me.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply accepts the invitation politely and asks a simple follow-up question. The other options are unnatural or do not fit the invitation.

sourceText: 我想邀请你去音乐会，因为我喜欢这个歌手和这支乐队。
acceptedAnswers: I want to invite you to the concert because I like this singer and this band.
I'd like to invite you to the concert because I like this singer and this band.
I want to invite you to the concert because I like this singer and the band.
hint: 注意用 because 表示原因，用 and 连接并列信息。

sentence: The movie was so ______ that I wanted to watch it again.
hint: -ing 形容事物的特征；-ed 形容人的感受。这里说电影让人想再看一次，所以用 exciting。

targetSentence: I was bored because the TV programme was boring.

sourceText: 那部电影很无聊，我看得都快睡着了。
options: That movie was boring, and I almost fell asleep.
That movie was amazing, and I almost fell asleep.
That movie was exciting, and I almost fell asleep.
That movie was bored, and I almost fell asleep.

audioText: 听发音：/ˈbɔːrɪŋ/
hint: 形容事物让人感到无聊的；不是说人感到无聊。

audioText: I was bored because the TV programme was boring.
question: 说话的人感觉怎么样？

scenario: 周末，你的朋友说他/她在电视上看了一个节目，但觉得很无聊。你想礼貌地回应并表示理解。
options: Oh, that sounds bored. I would be boring too.
Oh, that sounds boring. I would be bored too.
Wow, that sounds fantastic! I want to watch it again.
pragmaticsNote: Option 1 is correct because boring describes the programme, and bored describes the person's feeling. Option 2 uses the adjectives incorrectly. Option 3 is not a good response because it does not match the friend's feeling that the show was boring.

sourceText: 这部电影很无聊，我觉得很无聊。
acceptedAnswers: This movie is boring, and I am bored.
The movie is boring, and I'm bored.
This movie is boring, and I feel bored.
hint: 注意：boring 描述电影的特点，bored 描述人的感受。

sentence: I read ___ because it gives me information.
hint: 用一般现在时描述习惯；because 用来说明原因。

audioText: I watch videos online and read a blog because I want information and news.
question: 他在网上做什么？
options: 他看视频并读博客，因为他想了解信息和新闻。

sourceText: 我经常在网上看视频，也会读博客，因为它们给我很多有用的信息。
options: I often watch videos online or read blogs because they give me a lot of useful information.
I often watched videos online and read blogs because they gave me a lot of useful information.
I often watch videos online, but I read blogs because they give me a lot of useful information.
I often watch videos online and read blogs because they give me a lot of useful information.

audioText: 请听发音 /blɒɡ/：一种在网站上发布文章的网页日志。
hint: 你可以在网上读它，也可以自己写它；它通常是一篇一篇发布的。

targetSentence: I watch videos online and I read blogs because I like news.

sourceText: 我常常在网上看视频，也读博客，因为我想了解新闻。
acceptedAnswers: I often watch videos online and read blogs because I want to get the news.
I often watch videos online and read blogs because I want the news.
hint: 用一般现在时表达习惯；注意用 and 连接两个动作，用 because 说明原因。

scenario: 你和一位新同学聊天。对方问你平时在网上看什么，以及你为什么喜欢这些内容。你需要自然地回答。
options: I read news online, but I do not want information.
I am busy now, and I will talk later.
I read news online because I want information, and I watch videos because they are fun.
pragmaticsNote: The best response answers the question directly and gives reasons with because.

audioText: I have a bad tooth. I need a doctor.
question: 他说哪个部位不舒服？

audioText: tooth
hint: 听到这个单词的发音并根据中文释义输入：牙齿

sourceText: 我有一颗坏牙。我感觉很糟糕。我需要看医生。
acceptedAnswers: I have a bad tooth. I feel terrible. I need to see a doctor.
I have a bad tooth and I feel terrible. I need to see a doctor.
I have a bad tooth. I feel terrible, and I need to see a doctor.
hint: 用 have 表达身体不适，例如 I have a bad tooth。句子要简单、自然。

scenario: 你在诊所看医生。医生问你哪里不舒服。你想说你有一颗坏牙，而且你觉得很累。
options: I have a bad tooth and I feel tired.
I have a bad tooth, so I do not need a doctor.
I am a bad tooth and I feel tired.
pragmaticsNote: Use have to describe the health problem directly. The best reply is clear, simple, and appropriate for telling a doctor how you feel.

sourceText: 你应该每天吃水果和蔬菜，并且多做运动。
options: You must eat fruit and vegetables every day and exercise more.
You should eat fruit and vegetables every day and exercise more.
You should eat fruit and vegetables every day and rest more.
You have to eat fruit and vegetables every day and exercise more.

audioText: You should eat fruit and vegetables every day. You should drink water and have a healthy breakfast.
question: 他说了什么健康建议？
options: 你应该每天吃水果和蔬菜，喝水，并吃健康的早餐。
你必须每天跑很远的路。

scenario: 你的朋友最近总是不吃早餐，还常吃很多零食。你想给他一个简单的健康建议。
options: You should eat breakfast and fruit.
You must eat candy every day.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply gives simple, polite advice with should. The other options are too strong or unhealthy.

audioText: 请听发音并根据释义拼写单词：表示“健康的”
hint: 这是一个形容词，常用来描述人、食物或生活方式。比如：You should eat healthy food.

sourceText: 你应该吃健康的食物。
acceptedAnswers: You should eat healthy food.
You should eat healthy foods.
hint: 用 should 表示建议；healthy food 表示“健康的食物”。

targetSentence: It is raining today, so take an umbrella.

sourceText: 今天很冷，请穿暖和的衣服。
options: It is raining today. Please take an umbrella.
It is hot today. Please wear a hat.
It is cold today. Please wear warm clothes.
It is sunny today. We can go outside.

sentence: It is raining today. Please take an ______.
hint: 英语里可以用 It 来描述天气。这里句子说今天在下雨，所以空格里要填 umbrella，表示“雨伞”。

audioText: It is raining today, so I need an umbrella.
question: 听句子，选出正确的意思。
options: 今天很热，所以我需要一把伞。
今天在下雨，所以我需要一把伞。
今天很冷，所以我需要穿短袖。

audioText: 听到的发音是 /ʌmˈbrelə/，请拼写表示“雨伞”的英文单词。
hint: 提示：下雨天可以带它。

scenario: 你今天早上要出门，外面正在下雨。朋友问你应该带什么。
options: Yes, take an umbrella and wear a jacket.
No, it is hot today, so wear shorts.
pragmaticsNote: The best response matches the rainy weather and gives useful advice about an umbrella and clothes.

sourceText: 今天下雨了，你应该带伞。
acceptedAnswers: It is raining today. You should take an umbrella.
It's raining today. You should bring an umbrella.
It is raining today, so you should take an umbrella.
hint: 注意用 it 表示天气；可以写成 It is raining today. You should take an umbrella.

audioText: 发音：/ˈdeɪndʒərəs/。释义：形容动物很危险，可能会伤害人。请拼写这个单词。
hint: 这是一个形容词，意思是“危险的”。注意拼写：d-a-n-g-e-r-o-u-s。

scenario: 你和朋友在看一张蛇的图片。朋友问你："How do you describe the snake?" 你想说它很危险。
pragmaticsNote: The best response directly describes the animal with the correct adjective. The other options are understandable, but they do not fit the situation.

sourceText: 老鼠很小。
acceptedAnswers: The mouse is small.
A mouse is small.
hint: 把“老鼠”译成 mouse，把“很小”译成 small。

targetSentence: I want to visit the park because it is sunny.

sourceText: 我想去河边散步，因为天气很好。
acceptedAnswers: I want to walk by the river because the weather is nice.
I want to go for a walk by the river because the weather is good.
I want to take a walk by the river because it is nice today.
hint: 用 because 连接原因。前半句说想做什么，后半句说为什么。

audioText: I want to visit the park because it is sunny. We can walk and look at the trees and flowers.
question: 他为什么想去公园？

sourceText: 我想在公园里散步，因为今天很晴朗。
options: I want to walk in the park because it is sunny today.
I want to travel to the village because it is sunny today.
I want to visit the mountain because it is rainy today.
I want to walk by the river because it is cloudy today.

audioText: 听发音 /bɪˈkʌz/，意思是“因为”；请拼写这个单词。
hint: 这个词用来说明原因，比如“我想去公园，因为天气晴朗。”

scenario: 你和朋友在说周末的天气。外面是晴天，朋友问你想去哪儿玩。你想说你想去公园，因为天气很好。
options: I want to visit the mountain because it is rainy.
I want to travel to the sea because I do not like parks.
I want to visit the park because it is sunny.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply gives a clear outdoor plan and a suitable reason with because.

pairs.left: We were at home last night.
They were happy during the weekend.

question: 根据听到的内容，哪一个选项最符合说话者的意思？
options: 说话者觉得这部电影很受欢迎，因为它很贵。
说话者觉得这部电影很棒，因为它很有趣。
说话者觉得这部电影很无聊，因为它很长。

scenario: 你和朋友看完一部新电影。朋友问你：“你觉得这部电影怎么样？” 你想礼貌地表达你的观点并说明原因。
options: I think it is great because the story is interesting.
I believe it is boring because it is popular.
I think it is great, however I do not like the story.
pragmaticsNote: The correct response states an opinion and gives a clear reason with because. The other options are less natural because they are contradictory or do not give a suitable reason.

targetSentence: On Saturday I went to a festival with my friend.

sourceText: 昨天晚上，我和我的朋友们一起去了一个节日。我们和大家聊天，吃了东西，还跳了舞。我很开心。
options: Last night, I went to a festival with my friend. We talked with everyone, ate something, and dance. I was very happy.
Last night, I went to a festival with my friends. We talked with everyone, ate something, and danced. I was very happy.
Last night, I go to a festival with my friends. We talked with everyone, ate something, and danced. I was very happy.
Last night, I went to a party with my friends. We talked with everyone, ate nothing, and danced. I was very happy.

audioText: Last night, I went to a party with my friend. Everyone danced and sang. I had a great time.
question: 他说昨晚和谁一起去了聚会？

audioText: 听音并拼写这个单词：意思是“节日、庆典”。
hint: 这是一个名词，常用来谈论文化活动或节日聚会。它的拼写以 f 开头，请写出这个单词。

sentence: I went to a party with my friend ____ and talked with everyone.
hint: 表示在具体某一天，星期前面用 on，如 on Saturday。

scenario: 昨天晚上，你的朋友告诉你："我和一群朋友去参加了一个文化节。我们一起跳舞、吃东西，还见到了很多人。" 你想给出一个自然、友好的回应。
options: That sounds fun! I'm glad you had a great time.
Why did you go there yesterday night?
pragmaticsNote: The best response is warm and interested. It fits a friendly conversation about a party or festival experience. The other options sound unnatural, too direct, or rude in this social situation.

sourceText: 昨晚，我和我的朋友一起去了一个聚会，我很开心。
acceptedAnswers: Last night, I went to a party with my friend, and I was very happy.
I went to a party with my friend last night, and I felt very happy.
Last night, I went to a party with my friend and had a great time.
hint: 注意使用过去时间表达，如 last night，并把动词写成过去式，如 went, was, had。

sentence: I liked the movie. ______, it was a little long.
hint: ‘however’ 表示转折，常用于前后意思相反或有对比的句子；‘so’ 表示结果，前后是因果关系。

audioText: Yes, I agree. The topic is interesting, so we can discuss it more. However, I think this example is a little different.
question: 说话人对这个话题的态度是什么？
options: 他同意，并且想继续讨论。
他不同意，并且不想讨论。

sourceText: 我同意你的观点。不过，那部电影太长了，所以我有点累。
options: I disagree with you. However, the movie was too long, so I felt a little tired.
I agree with you. However, the movie was short, so I felt a little tired.
I agree with you, and the movie was too long, so I felt a little tired.
I agree with you. However, the movie was too long, so I felt a little tired.

targetSentence: I agree with you, so I also like this topic. However, the example is different.

audioText: 听到的单词表示“同意；赞同”，发音类似 /əˈɡriː/。
hint: 这是一个表示“同意”的常用动词，和朋友讨论时可以用它来表示你同意对方。

scenario: 你的朋友说：“I think summer is the best season.” 你不同意，但想礼貌地说明原因，并给一个例子。
options: Yes, I agree. Summer is hot and I like it.
I understand. However, I like spring better because it is cooler, so I can walk outside.
No, that is a bad idea. You are wrong.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply is polite, shows disagreement with however, and gives a reason with so.

sourceText: 我喜欢这个话题，不过我的朋友觉得它太难了。
acceptedAnswers: I like this topic. However, my friend thinks it is too difficult.
I like this topic; however, my friend thinks it is too difficult.
I like this topic, but my friend thinks it is too difficult.
hint: 注意用 however 表示转折；也可以用 but。句子前后内容相反，表达不同意见。

targetSentence: What does this phrase mean?

sourceText: 你知道课程信息在网站的哪一部分吗？
options: Do you know which part of the website has the course information?
Do you know how the course information on the website works?
Do you know when the course information is on the website?
Do you know who has the course information on the website?

sentence: ____ you know the meaning of this phrase?
hint: 这是一般现在时的一般疑问句，主语是 you，所以句首用 Do。

audioText: 听发音：/ˈmesɪdʒ/。意思是：信息；留言。
hint: 这是一个名词，常用于网站、电话或留言中，表示“信息”或“留言”。请拼出这个单词。

audioText: Excuse me, do you know where the class information is? It is in the Courses section on the website.
question: 录音中说课程信息在哪里？
options: 在网站的 Courses 部分

scenario: 你在学校网站的通知里看到一个课程信息，但有一个词组你不懂。你想礼貌地向老师问这个词组的意思。
pragmaticsNote: 在查找信息时，询问词组意思最合适；另外两个选项虽然是正确问句，但不符合当前需要。

sourceText: 你想问：哪里可以找到课程信息？
acceptedAnswers: Where can I find the course information?
Where is the course information?
Where can I find information about the course?
hint: 用 where 开头来问地点；也可以用 Where can I find...? 来礼貌地询问信息。

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend

pairs.left: We are going to meet tomorrow.
We are going to travel together.
Then we can go to the station.
If I am busy, I will go on the weekend.
pairs.right: 如果我很忙，我会在周末去。

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend
audioText: I'm going to travel to the station tomorrow. Let's meet there at the weekend. If you are busy now, we can wait until 9 o'clock and then go together.
question: 他们打算在哪里见面？

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend
targetSentence: We are going to meet at the station this weekend and then travel together.

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend
scenario: 你和同伴在聊天。你们计划明天在火车站见面，然后一起去旅行。对方问：我们几点见面？如果你很忙，我们还能改时间吗？
options: I am busy now, so I do not know. You can go first and I will think about it next week.
Let us meet at the station soon, or maybe not. Then we can decide after the weekend.
We are going to meet at the station at 9 tomorrow, and then we can travel together. If I am busy, we can meet later.
pragmaticsNote: The best reply gives a clear plan for time, place, and a backup option, using be going to and then.

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend
sourceText: 我们明天要在车站见面，然后一起去旅行；如果你很忙，我们可以等到周末。
options: We are going to meet at the station tomorrow or travel together, and if you are busy, we can go soon.
We are going to meet at the station tomorrow, then you travel alone, and I am busy until weekend.
We are going to meet at the station tomorrow and then travel together; if you are busy, we can wait until the weekend.
We meet tomorrow at the station and travel together, but if you are busy, we wait weekend.

unitTheme: Plan a Journey and a Weekend

Sorry, this machine costs too much. Can I help you?
Sorry, this machine is broken. Could you help me check it?
Sorry, this machine is dirty. Can you buy a new one for me?

sentence: Sorry, the machine is ___. Could you help me?

audioText: Sorry, this machine is broken. Could I use the other one, please? Thank you for your help.
question: 这位说话者主要想做什么？
options: 他想使用另一台机器，并礼貌地请求帮助。

audioText: 听音：表示“对不起；抱歉”的礼貌用语。
hint: 这是在商店、洗衣房或学校里遇到问题时常用的道歉词，首字母是 s。

sourceText: 对不起，这台机器坏了。你能帮我吗？我想买另一种。
acceptedAnswers: Sorry, this machine is broken. Can you help me? I want to buy another kind.
Sorry, this machine is broken. Could you help me? I want to buy a different kind.
Sorry, the machine is broken. Would you help me? I want to buy another one.
hint: 注意用 sorry 表达道歉，用 can / could / would 表达请求帮助；another kind 和 a different kind 都可以。

scenario: 你在学校洗衣房发现洗衣机坏了，衣服还是很脏。你想礼貌地向管理员说明情况并请求帮助。
options: Sorry, the machine is broken. Could you help me, please?
Thank you for the machine. It is very cheap.
Give me a new machine now.
pragmaticsNote: The correct response clearly says sorry, explains the problem, and uses a polite request with could you. The other options are rude or do not fit the situation.

targetSentence: I write an email because I need help.

sourceText: 老师，明天我不能去上课，因为我需要去看医生。谢谢您。
options: Teacher, I can't come to class tomorrow because I need to see a doctor. Thanks.
Teacher, I can come to class tomorrow because I need to see a doctor. Thanks.
Teacher, I can go to class tomorrow and I need to see a doctor. Thanks.
Teacher, I can't come to class tomorrow but I need to see a doctor. Thanks.

sentence: I cannot come to school today ____ I am sick.
hint: 表示原因时用 because，意思是“因为”。and 表示并列，but 表示转折，for 这里不适合这个简单句。

audioText: Hi, Ms. Green. I can't come to class today because I am sick. I will write an email and tell you more. Thanks for your help.
question: 他说这段话的主要目的是什么？

audioText: 听发音，拼写这个单词：表示“因为”，用来说明原因。
hint: 这个词常用于邮件和留言里，表示原因。答案只有一个，拼写时注意中间是 because，不要漏掉字母。

scenario: 小明今天生病了，不能去上课。他想给老师发一封简短电子邮件，说明原因，请老师发今天的作业，并表示感谢。请选择最合适的邮件内容。
options: Hi Ms. Lee, I can't come to class today because I am sick. Can you send me the homework? Thanks.
Hi Ms. Lee, I can't come to class today but I am very happy. Please give me more homework.
Hi Ms. Lee, I am sick and I will come tomorrow because I want homework. Thanks for help.
pragmaticsNote: The best email gives a clear reason, a polite request, and thanks.

sourceText: 我今天不能去上课，因为我有点不舒服。请帮我告诉老师，谢谢。
acceptedAnswers: I can't go to class today because I feel a little sick. Please tell the teacher. Thanks.
I cannot go to class today because I am a little sick. Please tell the teacher, thanks.
I can't go to class today because I am not feeling well. Please tell the teacher. Thanks.
hint: Use because to give the reason, and use please to make the request polite. Thanks can end the message.

pairs.left: I get up early every day.
I am going to build a modern house.
pairs.right: 我打算建一座现代的房子。

sentence: I usually teach my sister at home, and next month I am going to ______ a special game for her.
hint: be going to 后面要用动词原形，所以选 build。

targetSentence: I usually work hard, but yesterday I built a game, and next week I am going to teach it to my friends.

sourceText: 我平时每天早上七点起床。去年，我第一次参加了学校演出。明年，我打算继续努力，成为一名好老师。
options: I usually get up at 7 a.m. every morning. Last year, I took part in my school show for the first time. Next year, I’m going to work hard and become a good teacher.
I am usually getting up at 7 a.m. every morning. Last year, I participate in my school show for the first time. Next year, I will work hard and become a good teacher.
I usually got up at 7 a.m. every morning. Last year, I take part in my school show for the first time. Next year, I go to work hard and become a good teacher.
I usually get up at 7 a.m. every morning. Last year, I was taking part in my school show for the first time. Next year, I am going to work hard and becoming a good teacher.

audioText: 听录音并拼写这个单词：/ˈfeɪ.məs/。意思是“有名的；出名的”。
hint: 这个词常用来描述一个人、地方或作品很出名。

scenario: 在课程结束口语展示后，同学问你："Are you going to build a special game for your project?" 你想用简短回答说“是”。
pragmaticsNote: Use Yes, I am. for a going to question. Yes, I do. and Yes, I can. sound natural in other situations, but they do not match this grammar.

audioText: I usually teach my little brother after school. Last Saturday, I played a game with my friends and laughed a lot. Next week, I'm going to build a small robot.
question: 学生下周打算做什么？

sourceText: 我每天和朋友一起玩游戏。去年我玩了一个特别的游戏。明年我打算成为一名老师。
acceptedAnswers: I play games with my friends every day. I played a special game last year. Next year I am going to become a teacher.
Every day, I play games with my friends. Last year, I played a special game. Next year, I'm going to become a teacher.
hint: 先用一般现在时说日常习惯，再用一般过去时说过去经历，最后用 be going to 说未来计划。
